So this is kind of the next piece in that whole equation. It's a concept I call the value ladder. Who has ever heard me talk about value ladder stuff before? Good, good. Cool. So this is probably one of the most important things. If you want to take your company from making a little bit of money to making a lot of money, one of the other titles we kind of have for this session is how to outspend your competitors. Because that's really the purpose of everything we're talking about here. If I'm going head to head with somebody else, the person who can spend the most amount of money to acquire a customer is always going to win. For example, I look at our supplement right now. If you look at who our biggest competitor is, it's a drug called Lyrica. Anyone here hear Lyrica? You see them all over the TV, right? Now Lyrica's got billions of dollars in AdMoney they get from whatever. And I can't compete with them on TV. They're just thrashing us on TV because I can't spend as much to acquire customers then. It's impossible. They just burn through money. So we go online and we build the sales funnel where we can beat them. We are beating them online in every single channel because they're not coming out of those channels. But all of your business is always looking like, who are your competitors? Who are those people? And you have to create a business that you can spend more money to acquire a customer than them. And if you can do that, you're going to win every single time. Doesn't matter if their product's better. Doesn't matter if their customer service is better. Doesn't matter anything. If you can spend more to acquire a customer, you will always win. Now hopefully you'll also spend some extra money on good product and customer service. But the reality is that it's not the most important thing. Most important thing is that. And so the way we do that is when we're setting up our company initially and structuring it this way, is we have to understand this concept of the value ladder. So to begin with, I want to show you guys an example of this creepy clown. Anyone ever met him before or seen him before? Okay. I was trying to find the right picture for this slide. And I typed in Ronald McDonald. And then one of the suggestions was creepy clown. And so I clicked on that and there's all these pictures of Ronald McDonald killing things. And I was just like, I probably shouldn't put that on my slide. So this is what we got. So McDonald's is obviously a big company, right? And they understand how to advertise. They're on TV everywhere. They're spending insane amounts of money. And so what they understand better than anyone probably is the importance of the stuff I'm talking about. So if you look at their typical sales funnel, this is how it works with McDonald's. To get you to show up in their drive through, it costs them on average about $1.91. Is that interesting? So $1.91 advertising gets you to show up at their drive through window. Now when you show up at the drive through window, you place your first order, you buy a hamburger, which hamburger plus tax is $2.09. So when you look at that, you're like, man, they're not making much money. They're not making 18 cents for every person that goes through the drive through. Not that good, right? But McDonald's is a smart company. So this person comes in, they spend all this money, they acquire a customer, the person's there. Now they come back and say, hey, do you want fries with that? Do you want to cope with that? They upsell them and everything. And anything else they sell at that point is all just free money. Because they already broke even up here. They made a little bit of profit up here. But the real money is when they offer fries and a Coke for $1.77. Because from that $1.32 is pure profit to their bottom line. If you look at that, that's eight times as much money from the upsell. So without that upsell, they're nothing. The second they had that upsell in, they become McDonald's. And so for all of us, we have to understand that and look at our business. And look at how we can create that. So we're going to walk through the process. So you look at what we're trying to build out here over the next three days, you guys, something looks kind of like this. We're going to talk, oh, here's all the traffic, right? We're going to talk about that in the next session. Right here, this is my initial funnel. And the entire goal of my initial funnel is not to make any money whatsoever. If I do, that's awesome. If I make $0.18 like McDonald's did, that's awesome. That's not my goal. My only goal is to at least break even on this initial funnel. If I break even on this initial funnel, like I told you guys earlier, this is a million dollar year of business or more. But that's got to be our number one focus. Because after they've gone through and they've broken even on this front end funnel, now I've got a customer. I got the dude who just ordered the hamburger. All I do now is offer fries and a Coke and boom, I start making money. Okay, for us, our fries and our Coke is our continuity, our high ticket and our other offers we're going to sell. Because the second thing we sell them is 100% profit. For example, all you guys have seen are our sales funnel for this book, right? This is a free book we give away and we'll talk a lot about this strategy. But we give away this book for free. We have two other upsells. We have a $200 upsell and a $300 upsell. And for average, for every free book we give away, you look at the upsell process, we make about $40 in profit. Now a lot of people are like, sweet, you made $40 for every book you gave away. But the way I look at it is a little different. I look at it, I can spend $40 to give away a book. If I can spend $40 to give away a book, I broke even. My wife's like, why are you breaking even? That doesn't help me pay the bills. Okay, but I understand what McDonald's understands is that if I can break even, everything else is just profit. Okay, and so that's gotta be the goal of all you guys is we need to create an initial funnel with the sole purpose of breaking even and after that it's just pure profit. Now the cool thing is this book right now is costing us $20 to give away. On average, $20 to give away on Facebook. So we are making a profit on the front end of this, okay, which is awesome. But I can also go out to affiliates and we'll talk about this strategy in the next session. Now I could pay an affiliate and say, I'll pay you $20 to give away my book. I'll pay you $30 to give away my book. Or $40 or $50, and I can lose money giving this away if I've got this back and stuff in place. Okay, back when we had our big call center with 60 salespeople, our entire business model is we would give away free DVDs and we'd pay affiliates $60 to give away free DVD. You may look at me and say, Russell, you are moron. Why would you pay someone $60 to give away a free DVD? It's cuz I understood this process. I understood after I had that customer, I can make $100, $200, $500 on average from those exact same customers. Okay, the very first internet marketing event I was ever at, there was this guy named Mike Lipman that was speaking. Anyone here have ever seen Mike Lipman or met him or he wrote a book called Conversation with Millionaires, became a New York Times bestseller. And he was on the stage and super charismatic guy and he was talking and the one thing I remember him saying, he said that. And I don't know if everybody else in the room even caught or even understood it. I remember he said, he said, in business, amateurs focus on the front end. And when he said that, for whatever reason it just hit my head and everything else came quiet around me. I remember just having that epiphany, just amateurs focus on the front end. I kept saying amateurs focus on the front end. I kept thinking about that. I was like, man, that's pretty profound. The real people that are making good money in this business are focusing on the back end. When you understand, you understand that the entire goal of your first funnel is just to break even and you have the other things in place to sell. Now you've got the foundation, the structure you need to build a big business. Okay? All right. So again, whoever can spend the most money to acquire customer wins. All right, so the way we do this is through a concept I call the value ladder. That's what it looks like. Now a couple things to note on this value ladder. Left hand side here we have value. It's how much value we're offering someone. The bottom side is the price. Okay, this little dollar sign across the bottom is like symbolizes a continuity program where they're paying me all the time. And right here at the top, this is the best thing I have. Okay? When I look at creating something for somebody, I like to start in the very, very back end. We talked about before like who is the customer we want to serve, right? And then where do we want to take them? That's what we want to start like. What is the best possible thing you could offer somebody? How can you serve them at the highest level and help them the most? What is that? And so for example, I think the thing that we have to serve some of the highest levels what Robbie sold the other day for a million dollars. Okay, where Robbie and I fly to the office, I set up your entire funnel, I write the copy, Robbie trains the sales people. We set up ads like everything's done for you and you just walk away with this built in business. That's the highest level thing I could offer anybody. So if I was to come up to you on the side of the street, it's like hey Clint, I got this really cool package, it's a million bucks. You give me a million dollars, I guarantee I'll make you a million bucks. You in? A million for a million? Yeah, I guarantee it. Yeah, okay, so Clint's gonna say no. Why is he gonna say no? Cuz I'm just some dude off the side of the street comes up to him, right? I've never provided a value to him ever in my entire life. I've never actually helped him out at all, okay? So the way this value ladder works is that people, they like to kind of test the waters and like to see if people are what they're getting, right? And so the bottom down here, the value ladder, the value is really, really low and the price is low, right? And so I offer somebody something initially like that, okay? Good example is this book. Here's a free book from Russell, it's gonna show you 100 day of our proven split tests. Try it out. It's free, just pay five bucks, shipping, handling, it's yours. People are like, all right, I'll test it out. You get the book, they flip through it, excuse me. They're like, man, this is good. This is really good. I'm getting a lot of value from this. What else has Russell got? That's what they think, right? And then they naturally want to ascend up to the next level. They receive value at this point, that's how we are as humans. As soon as we receive value, we want more, right? You meet a cute girl, you give her a kiss, you receive some value, you want more, right? You keep pursuing her, you keep going through this process, right? I've been married for a long time, I'm pretty sure that's how it all works. It's the same thing in business. If you provide value at one level, people are gonna naturally send up to the next thing. And if you provide value at that level, what's gonna happen? They're naturally gonna send to the next, and to the next, and to the next, okay? All of you guys here are living proof that this concept works. Somewhere along the line, you receive some type of value from me, and you'll send it to the next thing and the next, and here you are in Boise, Idaho, hanging out with me, okay? It was all strategically built out, but that's kinda how the value ladder, how it works, okay? So a couple examples of this, you can see how they work in the real world. Some of you guys have real world businesses, so this might help. Probably about five years ago, we had our company going, I hired my first set of employees, maybe it was like seven years ago now. And I'd never had a real job before, I'd serve tables during the summers, but I'd never had a real job before. So I had all these employees, and one day someone's like, so what's our benefit package? I was like, what does that mean? Like, you know benefits were like, you give us stuff for free, like dental insurance and health, and I was like, I don't know, how's that normally work? And they were like, well, most bosses give you benefits. I'm like, all right, let's figure out how to do benefits. So someone went in and figured out how to get benefits, we had our benefit package, right? And so they gave me this dental card, so I hadn't had dental insurance since I got married, because I hadn't had a job. So I also had this dental insurance, I'm like, awesome, I have my teeth cleaned from dentists in like six years. And so I got really excited, and then one day we get this postcard in the mail from Willow Creek Dental, which is here locally in Boise, and they're like, hey, come in for a free teeth cleaning. I'm like, I got dental insurance, I got free teeth clean, this is gonna be awesome, right? So I call a guy up, we come in for my free teeth cleaning, the dentist is in there cleaning my teeth, and he's doing all the stuff that dentists do, and then after a while he's like, hey, just curious, do you smoke or drink coffee? And I'm like, what? He's like, yeah, do you smoke or drink coffee? Like, no, why would you say that? He's like, well, it's kind of awkward now, I just know your teeth are kind of yellow, I'm just curious, I assumed you smoked or drank coffee. I was like, my teeth are yellow, are you kidding me? He's like, it's not that big a deal, but if they're definitely yellow, if you want though, don't worry, we have this teeth whitening gel, these things we can fit in your mouth, and I'll put it in, and your teeth can come white again in a couple weeks. I'm like, oh, please yes, do that. So they did the, they made that little mold, you guys remember how they had that mold, they shove it in your mouth, and you're dragging on it, and you're trying to not die, pulls the thing out, and makes my little thing, and I'm like, okay, my teeth are white. He goes back in there for a little bit, and he's like, so did you ever have braces when you were a kid? Well, I get braces, he's like, yeah, I can tell. I'm like, how can you tell? He's like, well, people get braces, and they have them off for a long years, they start, the teeth start crowding back together to get all jacked, and we have to go, and you can kind of tell, and make, you can tell my teeth are like shifting back, like all messed up, he's like, well yeah, but I'm in your mouth, I'm like, what can you do about that? He's like, well, we have these retainers we can put on, you put them on at night, and then your teeth won't shift anymore, they'll kind of stay like that, or we got this really cool thing called Invisalign, where they'll shift them back to the way that they were. I'm like, okay, well, we definitely need to do that, because I don't want to get more ugly than I already am, like whatever we can do to like get those things, so they go and make me the retainers, and that's the next thing, and then, at the end of the thing, I walk out, and the lady at the front desk is basically, hey, I'll see you again in six months, and signs me up for the continuity program. So I walked in for my free teeth cleaning, and within 45 minutes to an hour, I walked out with $3,000 or $4,000 in bills. Now as I have said, do you think of the situation? No, I got valued every single, I got my free teeth cleaning, I got my whitening trays, I got my retainer, and luckily I didn't need any cosmetic dentistry, if I did, that's their high ticket, that's what would make their big money, right? And I got on a continuity program. Okay, that's how dentists make their money, and it's pretty cool, except for my wife went in the next week, and she got sold on every single one as well, so they made a crap load of money off us, but that's kind of how value ladder works in a traditional business. Think about the businesses that you interact with each day, and some of them are doing this, but the smart ones are doing this every single day. This is an example of our value ladder. We're rolling out a new version of this next week, and some of you guys will see, in fact, I'll probably show you guys some of it tomorrow, but the very front end, we're offering people as a free quiz, and I'm gonna talk to you guys about the power of pre-framing through quizzes, we're seeing some crazy numbers with it, but on the front end, we're gonna have a free quiz, so I'm gonna go through that, and receive a little bit of value, and then we can make, they want the next thing. Then from there, we're gonna upsell them a free book, which is right now, it's our dot com secrets live, it's our dot com secrets labs book, in the very near future, it's gonna be our, my dot com secrets book, Julie back here is actually writing my book right now, which is one of the main reasons why we're doing this event, this event is gonna become a book, and she's been writing it, and this will be the book that we upsell, it's like, hey, there's this really cool book called dot com secrets, free plus shipping, we ship it out to you, boom. And then after they've gone through that, then the next thing is we're gonna upsell them on this invisible funnel webinar, like, hey, you just got this book, I wanna invite you to this webinar training, we're gonna come in and train you on this whole process, they're gonna come in, they're gonna put their credit card in, they're gonna go through this training, at the end of it we'll build them if they like the webinar, and we'll talk about that strategy tomorrow, but that's the next thing. The end of that webinar, we're gonna sell them on the home study course, which is gonna be, guess what? The videos of this event, that's why we're making the videos. Okay, the home study course, and after the home study course, we'll upsell them into our inner circle, which is our $25,000 program, and then from there, we'll try to upsell them to the million dollar program, and that's our funnel. And then right here, click funnels, which we're launching very, very soon, is our continuity program. Every single person that comes in any step of this funnel, we're gonna try to offer them click funnels and get them building their funnels inside of our software, and we'll build them monthly for that. So there's our dot com secrets value ladder. So we'll talk about this for your business, for other businesses, because a lot of people, they set up a business, and they usually have one piece of this, or maybe two pieces if they're missing parts of it, and a really good example is my buddy, who's a chiropractor, he went through this exact same process with me, and I was trying to help him grow his company, and when I first met him, I was like, what are you doing? He's like, yeah, well, people come in, and we adjust them. I'm like, okay, what do you charge for that? He's like, I charge 50 bucks for an adjustment. And then what? He's like, that's all we do. We bring people in, I adjust them, and then they come back two days later, and we just do that until they stop coming in. Like that's kind of like the chiropractic model, right? Any chiropractors in here? Okay, that's kind of the model, right? The adjustments were, the 90% of chiropractors kind of live and die on that model. And so, one day, back, some of you guys know, like two years ago, two or three years ago, we built a wrestling team out here, and we had a bunch of guys come out, we started training them all, and doing some really cool stuff with it, and they had a chiropractor who would come in a couple times a week, and adjust all the guys on the wrestling team. And one day, the chiropractor couldn't come in, and all the guys were like in pain, and so, this is my buddy Brandon right here. Brandon's brother, Justin, was on the team, and Justin's there, and he's like, well, everyone's sore. He's like, it doesn't look that hard, but chiropractors do. I think I can figure it out. So we opened up YouTube, how to give a chiropractor adjustment. He watched about five minutes of video. He's like, all right, I get it. He walked over and adjusted everybody on the team. And so, Justin calls and tells me that, I start laughing. I'm like, man, this is gonna be really fun to call my buddy and make fun of him. This is something I can tease him about. So, I call up my chiropractor buddy, and I'm doing this as a friend, and so, I'm not trying to be mean to him, but I called him up, and I kind of told him the experience. I was like, dude, this is what Justin just did, and I said, how does it make you feel to know that all of the medical school you went to, he was able to figure out five minutes on YouTube and do everything that you're currently doing in your practice. And I started laughing. He gets pissed, right? He's just like, well, blah, blah, blah, blah, and he starts trying to get all defensive and all this stuff, and all the things he knows they don't know, and I said, chat, chat, just back up really quick. I'm like, I'm not doing this to be a jerk. I kind of am being too big a jerk, but this is a teaching moment for you. I wanna share something with you. I was like, when you're in chiropractic school, what other stuff did you learn? He's like, well, I learned how to do nutrition and wellness and this, and all these things you started mentioning to me. I was like, when do you ever offer that to your patients? Have you ever offered those things to your people? He's like, no. I'm like, well, do you think somebody comes in for an adjustment? Could they benefit from any of that kind of stuff? Any of the other value you have? He's like, yeah. I was like, well, why don't you offer that? He's like, you know, that's a good point. You know, I spent six years or however many years that he spent in school to learn this stuff, and he never offered the value that he could give people. Okay, I think a lot of us are the same way. We come in and you have something, and there's so much more you could offer somebody, and you know so much more, and you could do so much more, but for whatever reason you don't. Okay, for Chad, he didn't even think it, never crossed his mind once. Okay, and for some of you guys, it probably hasn't either. Like, what other value can you provide some? Somebody who just came in your clinic, you just adjust him. How else can you help this person? How else can you serve them? You start thinking about that, all of a sudden it opens up this whole new world of things on the back end. Okay, and so for him, we came back down and started trying to figure out some stuff, and he ended up building out a wellness program for $5,000 that he sells to people that come in. He all of a sudden would take and make $50 on an adjustment, he would have to have what, 100 adjustments? If he gets one out of those 100 people that go and buy his wellness program, he doubled his money. One out of 100, do you think that's hard to do? If you're really bad, it sells me one out of 10. If you're, yeah, if you had a flyer on your desk, you should get one out of 100. So, boom, also you double your money just by offering more value to your clients. Okay, no one's gonna be upset, they're all gonna be happy. Now the other side of that is, is the adjustments kind of like a middle of the value ladder type product, okay? Some of you guys have probably like $500, and that's my product, it's like, okay, that's a good product, but you've gotta get somebody to a certain level to be able to have them willing to spend that much money. Like what do you have on the front end? Like you need to step down and provide, not provide less value, but provide something that's smaller that's gonna get somebody to get somebody to take action faster. So for him, I was like, the hard thing about adjustments is like it's just not sexy. You think about like a medical doctor, like usually you have to go to medical doctor because you have to, you're gonna die. Okay, and then the other side of, then you have chiropractors in the middle, then you got like the masseuses we go to because it feels really good and we enjoy it, right? And they're kind of in the middle where it's just not, it doesn't really feel good, and you're like, you're not gonna die if you don't go, it hurts, but you're kind of in the middle, I'm like, it's just, it's like the hardest product in the world to sell. I said, we gotta figure out something that's sexy that's gonna bring people actually in the front door. And so he's developed multiple different things to bring people in the front door. He's done free massage to get people in the front door, he's done different wellness, it's a bunch of stuff, but something on the front end to bring somebody in and then you can send them up through your value ladder, does that make sense? Okay, so for all you guys, and why you think about your business right now, like what do you have right now? Like what do you have part of it, do you have all of it? Like which piece do you have right now, which piece do you not have? I promise you the most you guys are probably doing a little bit and you're missing out on the majority of the profits, okay? Here's a couple examples of people you'd be familiar with, like their sales funnels, okay? Tony Robbins, so anyone ever seen Tony's infomercials on TV? Do you get his infomercials, he does all the stuff and he's trying to sell you ultimate edge for just a dollar, I think it's a dollar trial and maybe it's free plus shipping. You come in and you get 30 days with it and after 30 days if you like it, he bill you $100 three times in a row. So $300 to get his ultimate edge. So that's his front end, now after you go through that, then the next thing to try and upsell you to is his first program called UPW, stands for Unleash the Power Within. That's where you get a comment, you get to actually walk on fire with him. Yes, you guys all should go ahead as well, that's like his foundational thing and he'll get three, four, 5,000 people in the room, three or four times a year, they all come in, you go through a four day event with him and then at day one you actually walk on fire, it's the coolest experience in the world, you go through everything and at the end of this event, and you've gone through, it's got so much value, at a pretty low level, 500 bucks to a couple thousand bucks, he tons of value and at the end of that, he upsells you on his master university, which gives you a date with destiny, a bunch of other things and depending on what you buy, you're gonna get anywhere from 5,000 to 10,000 dollars for that program. Anyone here who ever been to date with destiny? Couple of you guys, awesome as well. And you go to that program and then from there he upsells you to his live coaching, which is his continuity program, which is 2,500 bucks a month to have a live coach and then from there he upsells you to his platinum program, which is $60,000 a year and he keeps going on and on and on and on. That's how he does it in his business. Dan Kennedy and Bill Glazer, this is how GKIC works, you come in initially on a $50 a month membership, which is their gold program. After you get in, their entire thing they do is try to send you to their $250 a month silver program and their peak performers, which is 6,800 bucks a year, platinum is 25,000 and then titanium is 30,000. And what's interesting, if you look at, one thing I like when Bill used to run the company that was really interesting is that they have people each of these different tiers and what's cool is like, all of their cold marketing, they're trying to get people to join gold, all this traffic, all this stuff to get people into gold. And as soon as you're in gold, you no longer see all this stuff to get you into gold, all of a sudden they change and all the gold people they're marketing, how to become silver. Does that make sense? So all the people that are already here, they're selling out to the next level. And you get the next level, they sell you to the next. And so there's all these internal marketing programs happening at each level, where their goal is to send you from one to the next to the next to the next. Yesterday in our Mastermind Group, yesterday, I thought it was really cool with Scott and Garrett over here, they've got their software company, they bring people in their free trial, they're bottom the value ladder, someone goes through it and then they're trying to send somebody to a $30 month, all the way up to a $300 month and they call them on the phone and sell them a $3,000 package. And they're taking somebody through this exact same value ladder, providing value, and sending somebody up from the bottom to the middle to the top and keep moving them up. If you have people that are coming in on trials, like having either a call center or an email sequence or something to send somebody from this level to the next is one of the biggest things you can do to increase your business. Because anybody that goes from here to here and here to here, there's no new costs. All your cost was in getting somebody in the front door, now that they're there, it's completely free. You guys will see me after you've been in this kind of black box, this program, for about a year, my next goal will be to send you up to 25K group. And so that'll be, you guys will see the sequences that come out basically saying, hey, if you've enjoyed everything we've done this last year for you, here's the next level, here's the next step, here's the next place you need to be. It's all about sending people up. The first time I really saw this, I saw this whole concept in motion and saw the power of it, was when I joined their platinum group. Actually, it wasn't titanium, it was platinum back then and they added a new group later. But I did this group, I paid $20,000 to be in it, we're in a room with all these people. And my very first mastermind experience and it was awesome, it was so much fun. I remember when it was my turn to present my business, I showed them, I said, this is my model, I said, it's a free CD, and then I have a continuity newsletter and I sell people on a $5,000 back end. And I was so proud of my funnel, my value ladder and all this stuff, right, I've shown them off. I remember after I showed it, one of the guys in the room raised his hand, he's like, well, what do you sell the people that paid $5,000? Michael, nothing, they paid $5,000, that's my back end. He's like, Russell, I think you're missing the whole point. So the person that just paid $5,000 to be in the room, that's a $5,000 buyer. That's like the best buyer in the world, it's like you gotta be selling them the $10,000, and then the next piece in this value ladder. That's not like, who would ever, like no one's gonna pay me $10,000, $20,000, I never thought it was even possible. And then what was interesting is that night, so I just paid $20,000 to be at this event, and that night at the event, true to their word, they did exactly what they told me to do, and they pitched us on a $30,000 thing at that event, and of the 18 people in the room, eight of the 18 people who's paid $30,000 for the next thing that he pitched at the event. And I saw it in action, I saw, wow, this whole thing worked. Was it $30,000 extra? Yep, and the extra $30,000 to join this, I don't know if any of you guys saw the movie, the phenomenon that they put out, it was a huge flop, but they had people pay $30,000 to be in the phenomenon, and they sold a lot of them, they sold, like they said, eight from our group, and they sold a bunch from the other group, and probably made a million bucks just selling that. They're an existing group of buyers. And so it's something you gotta start thinking about, was like when you're bringing somebody in, and they've proven that they're a buyer, now you have all these internal things to ascend them up through different levels. One thing that I can and can he taught me, he said that a buyer is a buyer is a buyer. Somebody who buys something once is gonna keep buying things. Are any of you like that? Simon is definitely like that. I am, I'm like the worst person, Brent and Susan yell at me all the time, because I get excited about a topic, like six o'clock at night, and I buy something, and then I get excited and I buy everything I can find on that topic, and they come in the next morning, like Russell you spent $3,000 on all this weird crap, like what's going on? I'm like we asked you how I was interested in this topic, and they're like dude you can't just do that, but that's how people work, right? For example one day, this is a couple years ago, we took our team bowling, and bowling's like my third favorite sport, I love bowling, it's super fun, right? So we go bowling, and I'm like I'm not bad, I'm pretty good at bowling, and I'm very competitive in everything I do, and we're bowling, and one guy in our team, in our company beat me, and the guy's kind of like this cocky kid sometimes, especially when he knows it's gonna piss me off, and I was just like, and he beat me in bowling, and so for that whole rest of the night, he's just making fun of me, and I have my own balls, he's like oh you have your own ball, and you can't even beat me, and I'm just like, oh I'm so mad, right? And so that night I go home, and I'm at my computer, and I have this pain that's just like in my thing, so I'm pain, I'm trying to figure out like, how to become a better bowler, so I can call him up, we can go bowling, and I can beat him, right? And so the first thing I'm like, well my ball sucks, it's old, so I need to buy a new ball, and I start reading, and I'm like well most people is actually, good bowlers have two balls, there's one ball that's like your set ball, and there's one ball that's your strike ball, right? So I'm like well I need two balls, I buy two different balls, and then I was like, I had one of those gloves like the professionals have, but it was an old one, and I was like well, if I'm gonna do it right, I should get like a really good glove, so I buy one of the really good gloves, and then there's a couple like bowling DVDs of these masters, like how to do like the fishtail, literally within an hour and a half, two hours, I'd bought everything about bowling that I could find at that time, and I was ready to freaking beat this guy the next time we went out, and we never went out. In fact, I don't think outside of going bowling with my kids on their birthday, I haven't gotten bowling back since then, but for that little period of time, I was a buyer in heat, and I was excited, and I wanted to go through this thing, and that's what you're gonna find with your customers, you guys, is they get excited by something, and if you provide value at one level, they're gonna want more, and they're gonna want more, and they're gonna keep buying, and keep buying, and keep buying, and so you have to craft an engineer funnels that bring value in, and get people excited to keep getting more and more and more from you. Does that make sense? Okay, all right. So this, again, is kind of what your guys value ladder looks like. Now, in your books, what page are we on? You know, page 22, you can kind of see this graph here, and we're gonna do kind of like a little bit of a, I'm gonna let you guys work on this for a little bit, because this is important, and I want everyone to, not just conceptually understand, but I want you guys to do it. Now, one of the big things I learned the first time I started trying to build my value ladder is I learned that the hardest way to do it is to start at the bottom and work up, okay? So I was like, okay, I'm gonna build this value ladder. The first thing I need is something that's low ticket. So what's low ticket that people are gonna love? Probably a book, right? I'm like, sweet, that'll be awesome. So I start making this book, okay? It's been 10 years, I still have not finished my book. That's why I hired Julie to come in and write my entire book. It's so hard to write a book, right? And the thing about a book that's hard is the perceived value of a book, thanks to Amazon and Barnes & Noble is tiny, right? Most people pay for a book is 10, 20, 30 bucks, maybe for a book, and it's the hardest thing in the world to create, okay? And so what I did instead of creating this book, I reverse engineered everything, okay? And this is smart for you guys too. If you start in the back of the value ladder, it'll make your job a billion times easier. Okay, the first step I do is back here. What can I create that's gonna be the back end? So if I create that first, everything else will just kind of slide into place. For example, the very first time I did this thing, what I did is I did an event kind of like this, and I set up a camera and I got on stage and I started talking and I filmed the entire event, and the recordings that I've ever became a home study course we sold for $1,000, okay? It was kind of a middle of the value ladder. Then I took that home study course and I was able to pull out parts of that home study course on certain sections and I made that more of a middle, like a 30 or 40 dollar product. And I was able to pull out parts of those things and give away as a free plus shipping thing on the front. Okay, you'll understand more of this when we go through the funnels, but the easiest way to do it is to go backwards. Okay, the first time I showed someone this, I remember it was someone just like you who's sitting here in the audience and that was his big eyes, like, dude, that's so much better. So like, while we were on break, he went home and he called all of his, he called all his buddies back home and said, hey, next Monday I'm gonna be home, I'm doing, it was a finance guys, I'm gonna be doing a finance workshop at my house. You can bring you and one of your friends and look, about 10 people to show up, that'd be awesome. So he gets home from our event, the next Monday he shows up that night, he's got 10 or 12 of his buddies sitting in his house, he set up a flip chart like this, had a camera in the back of the room, and he started teaching, and he taught them for three or four hours, everything he knew about finance at the time, he took that recording and that became his home study course, he took one of the videos out of the most important thing, made that a front end, and launched a whole business in a day after he got home from our event. The hard way to do it you guys is to write a book and then create an audio program for that book and then create video, like, that way's so much harder, so much easier to start them back in and go forward. So all you guys have something that you know how to do right now, that's part of, you know, it would be your typical back end, and I think the easiest way to do that is really kind of start there and move forward, okay? I remember, when I was first getting this business, there's a guy named Matt Fury, he's kind of in the martial art wrestling niche, but he's also doing some stuff in the business niche, and so he launched this mastermind program where you could come three times a year out to Tampa and then he did monthly tele-seminars. And so the very first monthly tele-seminar I got and after I signed up, I was paying $1,200 a month, I was paying $1,000 a month or something like that. I'm on this first call and he's coaching everybody, he's talking about the value of doing your own event. So I'm listing this whole thing and then at the end of the day, he does Q and A, right? So the first guy gets on and it's this weightlifting coach, this guy named Brooks Kubik. Anyone ever, am I the only one listening to Brooks? Yes, he's awesome, huh? His stuff is so good. Anyway, this guy named Brooks Kubik's on the call, right? And Brooks has got this book called Dinosaur Strength Training, which is one of my favorite books I've ever read on strength training, it's really, really good. And so Brooks is on the call and Brooks is like, well hey Matt, I've got this book and I got like, I don't know, 10,000 people who bought this book but I don't know what else to sell them. And Matt's like, you know what you should do? You should put on an event for 8,000 bucks where people fly to your house and work out with you in your basement. And Brooks's like, that's a good idea, I bet you some of the people on my list, some of my buyers probably would actually do that. So Matt's like, hey, go do it and let me know what happens. Next I get this on the phone, next guy's like a runner or something, he's like, hey, Matt, this is my business so I'm doing like, what do you recommend I do? And Matt's like, you know, what you should do, you should go and like put on an event at your house where people can come and they can run your trails with you. Like that'd be awesome and you should charge $8,000 for it. And guy's like, that's a good idea. And third guy gets on, third guy's like, hey Matt, I'm an author, I wrote this book, like I can't figure out what to do next. And Matt's like, hey, you know what you should do? You should put on an event at your house and charge people A-ground to come there. And after the third guy was like, you know what I should do? I should put on my own event and have people come to my own house. Like that would be awesome. And so the next day I got up, I was super excited. At the time I had a list of maybe 5,000 or 6,000 people. I emailed my list, I'm like, hey everybody, I'm doing an event at my house, it's gonna be awesome, it's 5,000 bucks, this is how it's gonna work. And I had a PayPal button and I said, just click on this PayPal button and send me five grand and you come to my house. This how naive I was, I was like, I don't know how to even write sales letter this time. But I send this thing out, the next morning I'm sitting around, I'm waiting, I'm waiting, I'm excited. And like nothing came through and nothing happened. And I was like, oh, that Matt Fury's full of crap. That does not work at all. And so I'm all upset, we go to bed that night. Next morning I wake up, log in my PayPal account, $10,000 is sitting there. I'm like, you were there, BJ. And I was like, we just got $10,000 you guys. We had like two or three employees at the time, I'm like, we got $10,000. I was like so excited, two people had signed up that night. And so I sent another email, another email, I think we ended up getting three or four people that sent money, I was like just ecstatic. And then all of a sudden I had this feeling of horror come over me. I said, we sold four people, if they show up this event and there's only four people there, they're all gonna realize that they were the only ones that bought and that's gonna be really embarrassing. Who would have that fear too? I was just like, what am I gonna do now? So I'm like, I need some people to be in the room. So I call my friends, I'm like, hey, this is the deal. These four people gave me $5,000 to come hang out with me. Can you come hang out with me too and pretend like you paid money? Because I really need this to look like a lot of people signed up. And they're like, sure, I guess. And I call about 10 of my friends, I got 10 of my friends to show up, I did my very first workshop. And again, this is when I filmed, it became my home study course, it became everything else kind of branched off of that. And so for you guys, I would highly, highly, highly recommend doing the exact same thing. You all got talents and skills and things you're awesome at. And the fastest way to get out there is when you get home next week, call up a bunch of friends or whoever. You don't have to, even if you don't have anyone to sell to yet, don't worry about it. Just get five people, 10 people, who have people that would wanna know what you know, set up a camera and just record yourself and make it all happen. Now one of the big concerns I'm sure a lot of you guys are gonna have, cause I get this all the time, it's like, well what kind of camera should I have, what kind of this and all the, this is hopefully will be the biggest relief to you. Having nice quality stuff is good. That's why we got Brandon here who does an amazing job filming this whole thing. So I want really good quality. But the reality is that's not as important as you would think. For example, Brooks Kubic, the guy that I was talking about. So I bought his book and then he had these DVDs, it was $50 per DVD of him going out there and lifting weights and stuff. Teaching different things. BJ Smurking, because you know, BJ was my friend that was here. So I've been talking about this guy and all these things forever. And finally I was making some money so I'm like, I'm gonna order all Brooks's stuff. I haven't been wanting like 10 years to buy it, but it's $50 a DVD, which is a lot of money for me, right? So finally some money coming in so I buy like all six of these DVDs. So I spent, what's $50 time six, $300, right? They come on these DVDs and they showed up and BJ's at my house and Garrett, a couple of my buddies at my house and I'm like, you guys I got these DVDs. I was so excited, I was talking about them. I was like so pumped up. And so they're like, well let's watch them. Let's see what, like why are you so excited to watch these things? I'm like, all right. So we put the first DVD in, we sit on the couch, we push play and we sit down and we're all watching, just waiting and waiting and the screen's completely black. And all of a sudden you hear this woman's voice and she goes, hey honey, I can't see you. And like, look at each other like, what's going on? And then you hear his voice go, you gotta take the lens cap off, hun. And then you see him take the lens cap off and then walk back to the bench, sit down and lay down and start doing a bench press. And BJ and Garrett start laughing at me and then making, you spent 50 bucks on this crap. Like this guy didn't even edit out the beginning where he took the lens cap off. And I was so embarrassed. I was like, oh yeah, I'm gonna return, turn them off and like, so embarrassed, right? So he turned the whole thing off and they made fun of me for at least another, I don't know, two, three years. How many? Like it just kept going on and on and on. But that night after they left and my wife went to bed, I was like, all right, let's go see what Brooks has gotta say. Put the DVD in, push play and the attractive character of Brooks taught me. And for me, it was like the coolest thing in the world. I didn't care what it looked like. It was not what was important to me. What was important to me is Brooks, the attractive character, the guy who I had fallen in love with, the guy who I had listened to, I had read, I had listened, I had, the guy that I idolized, he had information for me and that's what I wanted. Okay, for you guys, that's the most important thing. That's the whole attractive character stuff. If you have that attractive character stuff, you can do so many mistakes and doesn't even matter. So I just wanted to tell that story guys again, here's a parable, right? Do you guys see how I'm practicing when I'm preaching? Anyway, so that was a parable to talk about just to help you guys understand the importance that the quality is really cool and great to have. It's not that important. What's important is you. It's your passion. It's your thing that you got that everybody else wants. You're super powered. That's what people are paying for. They're not paying for Hollywood production. They're paying for you, rock, unedited. I mean, we look at like right now, we are living in the YouTube generation. Like that's the quality people are expecting. If you can make a YouTube video and you have your passion come through, people will fall in love with you. And that's the key. That's really the key to the whole thing. Brand, can I make fun of you for a minute? Sweet, okay, so. I'm not just picking on you or you anyway. I'm picking on everybody here. So Brand, 10 years ago, 10 years ago this year, so he's my buddy, we wrestled together at BYU. So 10 years ago, I was kind of running this business and I was so excited about everything that was happening. I was making money and so I'm talking to him. He's like, I wanna make money. I'm like, well come on up man. So he drives up from Utah to Boise and we're hanging out. We spent a couple days and kind of went through like how to create your own product and stuff. And it was really, really cool. And then he goes back home to create his own product and he took $5,000. He hired this film crew to come and build this entire DVD of him doing all these body weight exercises. Which was a really cool product, really hot niche. Everything was perfect about it. We spent $5,000. It was 5,000 bucks, right? 25,000, 100? It's still a lot of money. It was all the money he had at the time, right? And spent it all on this thing. And he goes all this work, he's like, hey, my DVD's done, it's awesome. I'm like, okay, cool, let's go market it. He's like, I can't, I'm out of money. I was like, well you should have spent your money on the marketing, because that's what makes you money, not on the production of the thing, right? How many of the DVDs, have you made your $2,500 back yet? No. We will be selling DVDs at the back of the room. It's a really good product. But if I'm looking at my business, I've got $5,000 to invest, I'm gonna invest a lot less on the production and all of it on marketing, because guess what makes us the money, you guys? It's the marketing, right? And so anyway, that's just some stuff to understand, to keep you from holding you back from that. Because I promise you, that's gonna hold a lot of you guys back. It's just, it's gotta be perfect, gotta be perfect. It doesn't, okay? When you're doing this attractive character stuff we're talking about, it's you, it's your personality, it's your stories, that's the secret sauce. That's what people are gonna pay for. All right? Okay, so what we're gonna do right now is we're gonna spend a few minutes, and on page 21 and 22, I want you guys to kind of think about a couple things. First thing is whatever business you're in, and this works for any business. I've literally seen people that own pizza shops, who've created continuity programs and high ticket programs selling pizza. It'll work in any business, you just gotta think about it a little bit. So the first thing I want you to ask yourself is, in whatever business, whatever it is you wanna do, like what is the highest value that you could possibly provide somebody? What's the best thing you could do? Okay? If cost and price was not an issue, it was just the best thing you could do, what would that thing be? Okay, Dave and I were talking during lunch, do you mind if I share? I think it's awesome. So his whole brand is the backpacking businessman, right? So in the last three years, he's gone to 35 different countries and he's made the same amounts of money while he's just hanging out traveling from country to country, who here would like that kind of lifestyle? And so we've been brainstorming for the last day, like what's his funnel, what's the most important thing and today came to me at lunch, like I think I got it. He said, what I wanna do is like, his whole goal for people is to help them become free, like help them break the shackles of their job and all these things, where you have lifestyle, where you can literally do that and go and travel the world. So the best value I could ever provide somebody is, what if I did a program, we spend a year helping them break all the shackles, helping them replace themselves so they can get free and then they come and spend a month with me and we backpack around Europe and just have fun. Who here would wanna do that? That's exciting, right? How much value is that for somebody? That's his program. Think about for yourself, like what is the most value you could possibly provide somebody and that's gonna be your back end. And then what you're thinking about, kind of in the middle, like the way, well, the way I typically do is this. So I start with here at the back end, right? So what is that, what's that thing? So that's the first thing, I think that's one of the questions on here, right? Yeah, how can you best serve your dream clients? So I want you to think about that and think about what that is actually gonna be and I'm gonna have a couple of you guys share your thing afterwards. So if you think it's something really good, I want you guys to come up and share. So that's the first thing. Second thing I wanna think about the front end, like the bait, like what do you have that can get somebody in? Okay, now obviously there's gonna be middle level stuff, but my main thing that I wanna know is like, what's your front end gonna be and then what's the bait that's gonna get somebody, the initial, their initial, excuse me, foot in the door. And then the next thing is, what do you have monthly that you can provide somebody from a continuity standpoint? Continuity. Okay, a couple of ways to help you cheat on this. If continuity programs, there's a lot of ways to do continuity, but the best continuity program you guys can possibly have is not gonna be like a membership site where you get more information. Those things are good, but the problem is like, when somebody's bills start coming up and some issue comes, the first thing they cancel is their membership sites, they're just content. Okay, so you're always constantly adding fuel, adding fuel. I mean, I've run insane amounts of information product and membership sites and it's not very sticky, it's hard to keep somebody in. The best type of continuity in the world is software. Okay, look at Garrett, look at Scott, look what these guys are doing. They've got tons of people paying a monthly for software because there's a huge pain to disconnect. I would recommend for any of you guys that want continuity, think about how you can create some kind of software product to tie somebody into, okay, it's the best thing. Now, the reality is, if you guys create your software, right, and maybe we'll do a whole training on that sometime, but you can create really good software programs for a couple hundred bucks. Like literally two or $300, you can create a program that you can then sell for 50 bucks a month, okay? Like literally, I'm not even joking you. Like that's, the key is it's gotta be something simple that solves one big hurdle, one big task, one big issue that people are having. And you can hire someone in Romania or India for a lot less than you'd ever dream is even possible to create that product, okay? You can build big expensive programs too, but that's not what we're about here. About like what's the one thing I can do to really change somebody, okay? So if you can think of some kind of software for your business, I would look at that for sure. All right, now the other big hint I wanna give you guys, I shared this yesterday in the mastermind. When you're dealing with your clients, there's two type of things, right? There's things that they need, and there's things that they want. Okay, now I'm gonna tell you guys, the biggest problem that almost every entrepreneur has when they get started in this business is when they're trying to create like, what am I gonna sell, what am I gonna do for things? They come to me and they're like, hey Russell, this is exactly what my people need. They need me to show them this and this and this and this and this. It's exactly what they need. If you just said that and listen to your words, if you said this is exactly what they need, that is not a good front end product, okay? Never, ever, ever, ever, ever sell on the front end what they need. They will never buy it. That's what's screwed about humans, screwed up about humans. We never buy what we need, we always buy what we want. Okay, I think I've had three people in the last two days who were in the health and fitness market and they were like, I don't wanna give any kind of fad diet, any kind of crap. I wanna give people a certified plan to get healthy. Nobody wants a certified, they need that, you're right, they need to eat less. They really do need to eat less. They really do need to exercise more, they need it. But guess what? Nobody wants that, what do they want? Okay, they want the fad diet. So you gotta create some kind of fad diet to get them in the door and after they've come in the front door then you can give them exactly what they need but you have to create what they want or else you're never gonna make any money. That make sense? So what we look at, I always create on the front end is what they want and on the back end is what they need. I think that what everyone of my customers need is an experience like this where you can learn the entire system. If I was to sell us on the front end, nobody would ever buy it, is that weird? But I give you one little thing, I figured out what's the one thing that's the best in this entire system? I pull that one thing and I pull right here and boom, I get people in the front door and then after they're in the front door I can sell them exactly what they need but they will not buy what they need first. It's the key. And so what I would look at is the first thing we're doing is we're developing your back end programs. Your back end programs, what do they need? They need to come hang out with me for three days or for a month or for whatever thing is gonna be and this is where I'm gonna provide what we're gonna do and this is all the areas we're gonna help them with their health, their fitness, all the stuff that they need, right? That's what this is gonna be. And then from there you look at all these things right here and you gotta figure out like what's the sexiest thing of all of these? What's the one thing? You know one of my biggest all-hours over the last six months is this concept, the one thing. What's the one thing? Every time I create an offer and I think we'll talk a lot about this tomorrow, every time I create an offer though where it's like you're gonna get this and this this is gonna have the and, it becomes two things and conversions drop. You don't wanna ever sell two things on the front end. You want one thing. What's the one, most sexy, most exciting thing in the world? That one thing is what you wanna be focusing on. Can we go to our sell scripts? I'm gonna pound this into your guys' heads tomorrow or I think Saturday on the sell scripts is that the worst thing you do in selling is like is talking about you're gonna get this and this and this all this kind of stuff. It's all about like what's the one core thing that they want the very, very, very, very, very most. Okay? And that is what's gonna be over here. So for me, when I was creating our funnel for this, one thing people want the most, they don't want me to teach them how to do split testing. Okay, that's what they need. They need it bad. Nobody wants it. What do they want? They want me just to give them all my best stuff. Here's a hundred and my best test, just copy them. Sweet, that's exactly what I just wanna copy you. I don't wanna think, I don't wanna try to wanna do it. Okay, give what they want. After they're in the funnel, I sell them an event like this which is what they need. Does that make sense? Okay, I find the one piece is sexiest. I'm gonna need to look at a couple other businesses. Here's my network marketing front end. So network marketing gets what people want. For network marketers, what do you guys all want? Did you drill? Well, most of they want, they want leads and they want cash. Right, so here's my front end for my network marketing funnel. How I generated 7,489 paid members in my primary opportunity in just 45 days. Who here thinks that's what network marketers want? If I came to you and said, hey, I can show you how 7,400 paid members in 45 days you want in. Yeah, I want in. Boom, I give them that. This is what they want. They're in the door and I can sell them what they need. Let's see, our weight loss thing. This is our weight loss offer, the skinny bean diet. This is our fad diet. I believe in it, the concept, so I don't have any issues with it. But I pulled out the one sexy thing and it's this chocolate drink you drink that makes you lose weight. The one thing. And then they come in, we sell them all sorts of stuff on the back of it. But what's the one thing? So think about your business, if all the pieces you're gonna have, what's the sexiest thing? Yesterday in the mastermind group, we did this. We did this and wish a lot. I always blank out on your pen name from the stage. I'm so sorry. Shailoa. On her business, we went through this exercise and we said, what do you want to build? And she mapped out all the different pieces. Here's all the stuff I want to teach. Remember I said, I was like, okay, all that stuff is awesome, that's what they need, not what they want. And then from that thing, there's all these elements. I'm gonna teach people how I lost weight, how I get more energy, how I did this. And then one of the things she said, she's like, one thing I'm teaching is how I lost what's the numbers, 18, 15 pounds in eight weeks after she had her baby without doing exercise or anything just by drinking this drink. That was one of these things she kind of threw out there. And then everyone in the group were like, what's the sexiest thing? Every one of us did this accident, and they're like, boom, that's it. That's the one that's the hot bun, that's the sexiest thing. So right now she's taking that one piece out, boom, take it at the front end, and everything else goes through. That make sense, you guys? Okay, so what I want you guys to do right now, we're gonna spend probably 10 minutes on this, is I want you guys to think about this. Like what's your back end thing? What's the most value you can provide somebody? What do your customers actually need to give them the biggest transformation? That's your back end. The second thing is of those things, what's the sexiest thing I can bring out for my bait? What do they actually want the most? And then the third thing is, what can I offer them on a monthly basis? It's gonna keep them coming back for more. So membership size, it's software, what can I create that's gonna be, give me that residual income?
